problem_addressed: The paper targets the evaluation of machine learning agents on machine learning engineering tasks, specifically predicting molecular properties from molecular structures.
core_claim: MLE-bench provides a standardized benchmark framework for evaluating machine learning agents on machine learning engineering tasks with a focus on molecular property prediction.
key_contributions: A comprehensive benchmark framework (MLE-bench) for evaluating ML agents on ML engineering tasks
A structured evaluation metric (Log of Mean Absolute Error) that normalizes improvements across different scalar coupling types
A rich dataset with multiple molecular features including XYZ coordinates, dipole moments, shielding tensors, and Mulliken charges
novelty: The novelty lies in creating a comprehensive benchmark that evaluates both prediction accuracy and engineering capabilities of ML agents in handling complex molecular property prediction tasks with multiple feature types.
method_summary: The method involves training ML agents to predict scalar coupling constants between atom pairs using molecular structure features and additional molecular data, with evaluation based on a log-MAE metric averaged across coupling types.
method_components: Feature extraction from molecular structure files (XYZ coordinates)
Integration of additional molecular data (dipole moments, magnetic shielding tensors, Mulliken charges)
Log-MAE evaluation metric with floor at 1e-9 for numerical stability
key_innovation: The key innovation is the Log of Mean Absolute Error metric that ensures equal weight for 1% improvement regardless of scalar coupling type, calculated as the average of log(MAE) across all coupling types.
assumptions: Molecular structure files (XYZ) provide sufficient information for accurate property prediction
Additional molecular data (dipole moments, shielding tensors, etc.) are relevant and useful features
Test set molecules are completely unseen during training (molecule-wise split)
theoretical_grounding: The paper provides a mathematical formulation for the evaluation metric with a theoretical minimum score of approximately -20.7232 for perfect predictions, but does not include formal proofs or theoretical bounds.
headline_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3 ± 2.4% in Low complexity category
improvement_over_baseline: o1-preview outperformed other models with 16.9 ± 1.1% overall medal rate
best_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3 ± 2.4% in Low complexity category
title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
